quality_notes: Keep the post in the LinkedIn sweet spot: roughly 140 to 340 words.

post_text:
📌 LLM Architecture
Most engineers think Large Language Models like GPT-4, Gemini, and Claude are just massive neural networks with more layers and parameters —
they aren't: what actually makes them powerful is the internal architecture, carefully designed to handle complex patterns in language 🤖.
```
Internal Architecture:
1. Tokenisation - Raw text split into sub-word tokens
2. Embedding - Tokens mapped to dense vectors
3. Attention - Multi-head self-attention mechanism
4. Feed-Forward - Transform and project hidden states
5. Output Layer - Project to vocabulary, sample token
```
When building on top of LLMs, understanding this architecture is key — from Tokenisation to Output Layer, each component plays a crucial role in generating coherent text 📝.
💬 Which component do you think is most important for LLMs:
1️⃣ Tokenisation  2️⃣ Embedding  3️⃣ Attention  4️⃣ Feed-Forward  5️⃣ Output Layer
#LLMs #NaturalLanguageProcessing #AIArchitecture #DeepLearning #LanguageModels 💡👀💻
